title: rubus × coarctatifrons
intro: Rubus × coarctatifrons, a hybrid species of raspberry, is a cross between the black raspberry (Rubus occidentalis) and the thimbleberry (Rubus parviflorus). The medicinal properties of Rubus × coarctatifrons are attributed to its high content of anthocyanins, powerful antioxidants that have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and anti-cancer properties. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. The herbal preparations of Rubus × coarctatifrons typically involve drying the fruit and leaves to make teas, tinctures, and capsules, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
